expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: quality-scenarios.md (156 lines, 9 scenarios) and analysis.md (69 lines) both present with substantive content.

expectations.text: A1: Scenarios use ATAM format (Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure)
expectations.evidence: All 9 scenarios have all 6 ATAM fields: Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure (labeled 'Characteristic' as well).

expectations.text: A2: Diverse test types — at least 3 different types, not all fitness-functions
expectations.evidence: 4 different test types used: manual-review (3: QS-001, QS-002, QS-006), integration-test (4: QS-005, QS-007, QS-008, QS-009), unit-test (1: QS-004), fitness-function (1: QS-003).

expectations.text: A3: Style fitness functions NOT duplicated — no scenarios for structural invariants already covered by style FFs
expectations.evidence: Style FFs for modular monolith cover: circular deps, module boundaries, single artifact, schema per module. No scenario duplicates these. QS-003 tests independent test suite execution (testability), not module boundary enforcement. QS-005 tests change isolation (no cross-module test failures), which is a behavioral check different from the structural boundary enforcement FF.

expectations.text: A4: Tradeoffs identified — at least 1 explicit tension/conflict between characteristics
expectations.evidence: 2 tradeoffs documented: Audit Logging vs Simplicity (QS-008 audit completeness vs QS-001 onboarding simplicity), Testability Isolation vs Audit Log Coverage (QS-004 no external deps vs QS-008 audit persistence). Plus 1 sensitivity point on module boundary granularity.

expectations.text: A5: Summary table present — table listing all scenarios with test types
expectations.evidence: Scenario Summary table with columns: ID, Characteristic, Scenario, Test Type, Priority. Plus Test Type Distribution table.

expectations.text: A6: Response measures are CONCRETE — numbers, thresholds, or specific observable outcomes
expectations.evidence: All measures are concrete: 'First productive contribution within 5 working days' (QS-001), '0 cross-module test dependencies' (QS-003), '<30 seconds with zero network calls' (QS-004), '100% of protected endpoints return 403' (QS-007), '100% of data access operations produce an audit log entry' (QS-008), '99% of requests return 2xx during business hours, measured weekly' (QS-009).
